title: Cynanchum sinaloense
intro: Cynanchum sinaloense, also known as Mexican swallowwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum sinaloense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cynanchum sinaloense is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is native to Mexico and has been classified under the genus Cynanchum, which belongs to the Apocynaceae family. Cynanchum sinaloense is further classified under the order Gentianales, which also includes other families such as the Gentianaceae and Loganiaceae. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its trumpet-shaped flowers and hairy stems. Cynanchum sinaloense is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which comprises over 5,000 species of flowering plants.
